# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.

Prevent SQL injection.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php
$host = 'localhost'; // replace it with actual host name if needed  
$db   = 'test_database';    //replace by your database's realname or username and password (if necessary)    
$user = '';//username of the user in mysql 
$pass = '';//password for that specific MySQL User. If you have no access to change this, leave it empty ''  
$charset = 'utf8mb4'; //or use utf8 if your data contains non-ASCII characters and not latin1 or utf8_general_ci 

try {    
    $dsn = "mysql:host=$host;dbname=$db;charset=$charset";  
    echo $sql = $dsn."\n\n"; //prints the DSN string to see if it's correct. Uncomment this line for debugging purposes! 
    
    $opt = [PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE            => PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION,  
            PDO::ATTR_DEFAULT_FETCH_MODE => PDO::FETCH_ASSOC,   
           ];     
        $pdo = new PDO($dsn , $user , $pass ,  $opt);  //create a pdo instance. Uncomment this line for debugging purposes!  
        echo 'Connected successfully';    
} catch (PDOException $e) {    throw new Exception('Error: ' . $e->getMessage()); }//catch any error and print it out to the screen  //Uncomment these lines if you want more detailed exceptions.     
?>
